what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger card for the recollection project: it documents a refit clone of episodic-memory v1.4.2 that serves as the Company's recall tool, recording its purpose, components, dependencies, data relationships, and the evidence observed.
observations: Forked from obra/episodic-memory v1.4.2 and reworked into the Company recall tool.
Has its own git repository and is NOT a registered MCP server.
Written in TypeScript/Node as a CLI and library (recall.ts, recall-cli.ts, clone-cli.ts, indexer.ts, distill/, backfill.ts).
Consumes the embedding service at :8007 (pplx-embed-context-v1-4b lens) rather than serving it.
Provides scheme-addressed recall units: board:// and clone://.
Live data store is separate at dot-recollection (conversation-archive/, conversation-index/, logs/, self/).
Depends on company and relates to cache-company.
Classified as kind: engine, not data, because the code consumes the embed lens while the data lives elsewhere.
Coverage is very low: only 6 of 10430 files read.
status is unconfirmed but move_intent is marked done.
README still references the upstream episodic-memory install.
standouts: It is a client of the :8007 embedding lens, not the server of it.
Uses meaning-based recall with board:// and clone:// scheme-addressed units.
Own git repo, not a registered MCP.
Kind chosen as engine while its data is offloaded to a separate ledger entry (dot-recollection).
conventions: Uses terrain-entry YAML frontmatter with type, name, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, dates, size, coverage, git_remote, purpose, version, language, embedding_model, ports, data-of, depends-on, relates-to, secrets, move_intent, tags.
References related ledger entries with wikilink syntax like [[dot-recollection]].
Separates Observed evidence from descriptive claims.
Uses descriptive register rather than normative ranking.
concerns: Coverage is only 6/10430 files, so most claims are surface-level.
README still points to upstream install, suggesting the refit may not be fully self-describing.
embedding_model field names a consumed lens while ports lists 8007, which could be misread as a service this project serves.
notes: This ledger entry maps recollection as a Company recall engine that consumes an external embed lens and keeps its archive separately at dot-recollection.
questions: Which exact component serves the :8007 embedding lens?
What do board:// and clone:// schemes resolve to in practice?
Does the package.json version 1.4.2 reflect the current refit or leftover upstream metadata?
Which six files were actually read?
What does move_intent: done signify operationally?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it documents a refit clone turned Company recall tool and correctly notes it consumes rather than serves the embed lens.
apparent_themes: Separation of code engine from data store
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a canonical, evidence-backed map entry for a third-party-derived recall engine so the orienteering ledger can track its identity, dependencies, and data boundaries without conflating code with data.
novelty: The file explicitly classifies the project as an engine while placing its live data store in a separate ledger entry, and it emphasizes that the embedding lens is consumed rather than served.
contribution: Provides a clear boundary description for a memory/recall subsystem, separating the TypeScript engine from its data store and embed-service dependency, which helps orienteering queries reason about ports, ownership, and dependencies without inspecting thousands of files.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain-entry ledger card for recollection, a TypeScript fork of episodic-memory v1.4.2 acting as the Company's meaning-based recall engine over conversation archives via board:// and clone:// units, consuming a :8007 embedding lens and storing data at dot-recollection.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog recollection's identity, upstream lineage, runtime dependencies, and data boundaries in the Company orienteering ledger.
